characters.name: Figures in background (colonial settlers and Native Americans)

event: The Peaceable Kingdom — Isaiah's vision of universal peace
symbols: lion lying peacefully — the fierce predator tamed by messianic peace
lamb and kid beside predators — vulnerability safe in the presence of former enemies
children among wild animals — innocence leading the peaceable kingdom as Isaiah prophesied
leopard with kid — the reconciliation of natural enemies under God's reign
background gathering of peoples — the extension of peace to human relations
lush green landscape — Edenic restoration and the new creation
river — the waters of life and abundance in the messianic age
description: A naive-style painting depicting a group of wild and domestic animals gathered peacefully together in a green landscape. A large lion, a leopard, a bear, an ox, lambs, and other animals sit or stand calmly together. Among them, several small children interact fearlessly with the beasts. In the background to the left, a group of figures in colonial-era clothing appears to be gathered near a river, possibly in a scene of negotiation or treaty. The landscape features large trees, a river, and distant hills under a soft sky.
theological_description: Edward Hicks's Peaceable Kingdom is a direct visual exegesis of Isaiah 11:6-9, the messianic prophecy of universal peace: 'The wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the young goat, and the calf and the lion and the fattened calf together; and a little child shall lead them.' The painting faithfully renders each element of Isaiah's vision — predator and prey at rest, children safe among beasts that would normally devour them. This prophecy belongs to the 'Branch of Jesse' oracle (Isaiah 11:1-10), describing the reign of the coming Messiah under whom justice and peace will transform all creation. The vision reverses the curse of Genesis 3, where enmity was placed between creatures; here, Edenic harmony is restored. The background scene of colonial figures and Native Americans in peaceful assembly extends the Isaianic vision to human politics — 'they shall not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain, for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the Lord as the waters cover the sea' (Isaiah 11:9). Romans 8:19-21 develops this further: creation itself groans for liberation from bondage to decay, awaiting the revelation of the children of God.

scripture_refs.reason: The direct scriptural source: the wolf dwelling with the lamb, the leopard with the kid, and a little child leading them
The Branch of Jesse prophecy — the messianic king whose reign brings about the peaceable kingdom
The wolf and the lamb feeding together in the new heavens and new earth — the eschatological fulfillment
Creation groaning for liberation from bondage — the cosmic scope of redemption pictured in the peaceable kingdom
The original Edenic peace where all creatures were given plants for food — the state this vision restores
The new creation where there is no more death or mourning — the ultimate fulfillment of the peaceable kingdom
